danger_signals.visual_form: opponent movement toward player, attack animations
danger_signals.meaning: immediate threat requiring defensive action or counterattack

danger_signals.visual_form: health bar reduction during combat exchanges
danger_signals.meaning: player character taking damage; continued damage leads to match loss
count-down to loss; imminent match end if not risen in time

safety_signals.visual_form: player character positioned in center of ring with space from opponent
safety_signals.meaning: advantageous position; opportunity to attack or evade
temporary vulnerability window; safe moment to recover or setup next move

safety_signals.visual_form: wrestler locked in submission hold animation
safety_signals.meaning: dominant position; damage applied to opponent

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: WWF logo/branding on ring canvas and wrestlers
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: opponent wrestlers display unique sprite designs and color palettes distinguishing them from player
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: WWF WrestleMania branded ring setting; crowd audience indicates professional wrestling venue

faction_indicators.color_identity: distinct wrestler color schemes (sprite palette variants)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: each opponent wrestler has unique visual identity—different body type sprites, costume colors, move animations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: character name displayed above ring; wrestler entrance associates them with WWF brand

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: active ring combat with two wrestlers visible; health bars displayed; crowd visible in background; referee present
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI opponent continuously pursues, attacks, and responds to player actions; wrestler moves fluidly through grapple and strike animations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: wrestler sprite transitions to prone position on canvas; referee enters ring and begins count animation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: downed wrestler must press button to rise before referee count completes; opponent may perform follow-up attacks

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: opponent remains down as count completes; victory animation or pose displayed for player character
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: match ends; transition to next opponent or champion selection

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: player character remains down as referee count completes; opponent celebration or victory pose
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: match ends; player returns to roster selection or tournament bracket reset

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: ladder of increasing difficulty opponents; advancement through tournament bracket structure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: successive opponents become faster, more aggressive; AI applies more complex attack patterns

environmental_storytelling.example: player begins against lower-tier opponents (weaker AI, slower moves) before facing WWF champions; visual progression in opponent sprite complexity and animation fluidity communicates competitive tier

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: professional wrestling ring setting with ropes, canvas, corner posts, and crowd; referee officiating matches
environmental_storytelling.narrative_implication: establishes professional wrestling context; legitimizes combat as sport rather than street violence

environmental_storytelling.example: each wrestler has distinct sprite appearance and color scheme; player learns to identify upcoming opponent by visual design before name confirmation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: charges toward player, executes grapple or strike moves, maintains forward momentum
moves away from player, creates distance, executes defensive animations
remains prone on canvas; AI pauses attack behavior until attempting to rise
stands over downed wrestler with counting animation; countdown gesture toward player/opponent
executes moves matching controller input; recovery mechanics triggered by button press during knockdown

notes: Source material from TCRF provides minimal detail on actual visual communication mechanics. This extraction is heavily inferred from beat-em-up genre conventions and professional wrestling game standards of the era (1989 NES). The game communicates world-state primarily through sprite animation states, health bar feedback, referee positioning, and opponent movement patterns. Faction identity in WWF WrestleMania is expressed through individual wrestler differentiation rather than faction-vs-faction conflict; the 'WWF' itself is the unified league context. Visual danger signals rely on opponent proximity, animation state transitions, and health bar depletion. Safety signals emerge from ring control positioning and opponent downed state. The game's political situation (wrestler hierarchy, championship progression) is manifested through tournament bracket structure and opponent difficulty scaling.
confidence_notes: Extraction based on limited documentary evidence. TCRF article does not detail visual communication systems; inferences drawn from game genre paradigm (beat-em-up) and professional wrestling simulation standards. Actual sprite/animation analysis would require direct gameplay observation.
